halfLife.conditions: Lelo et al. 1986: theobromine plasma half-life 7.2 h (six healthy males, theobromine dosed directly). Consistent with the wider literature (Baggott 2013 psychopharmacology study and IARC review give ~6-12 h; point value 7.2 h, band 6.1-10 h). Longer than caffeine's (~4-5 h), as expected for a monodemethylated xanthine with lower clearance.

vd.conditions: Derived from Lelo 1986's directly-measured theobromine disposition: plasma clearance 1.20 mL/min/kg and half-life 7.2 h give Vd = CL.t½/ln2 = 0.072 L/h/kg / 0.09627 /h = 0.748 L/kg. This is an apparent Vd/F from oral theobromine, treated as a true volume under the apparent-volume convention (F = 1; theobromine is well absorbed). Scaled to absolute litres against the 70 kg reference subject at derivation time (~52 L).

F.conditions: Set to 1 by the apparent-volume convention (the Lelo clearance and volume are apparent CL/F and Vd/F from oral dosing, so F is folded in and not double-counted). Theobromine is efficiently absorbed after oral intake (Baggott 2013); the plotted curve is true plasma concentration C = F.D/V.

tmax.conditions: Baggott et al. 2013: peak plasma theobromine ~3 h after oral pure-theobromine capsules (2-3 h; a chocolate-based matrix peaks somewhat faster, ~2 h). Point value 3 h for the pure-compound capsule case; ka derived from this Tmax at derivation time.

lelo_pk.title: Lelo A, Birkett DJ, Robson RA, Miners JO. Comparative pharmacokinetics of caffeine and its primary demethylated metabolites paraxanthine, theobromine and theophylline in man. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 1986;22(2):177-182 (PMID 3756065). Six healthy male volunteers, each compound given orally: theobromine plasma clearance 1.20 mL/min/kg, half-life 7.2 h (caffeine 2.07 mL/min/kg / 4.1 h for comparison).

baggott_2013.title: Baggott MJ, Childs E, Hart AB, de Bruin E, Palmer AA, Wilkinson JE, de Wit H. Psychopharmacology of theobromine in healthy volunteers. Psychopharmacology (Berl). 2013;228(1):109-118 (PMID 23420115). Oral doses 250/500/1000 mg pure theobromine; peak plasma ~2-3 h; half-life ~7-12 h; dark chocolate contains ~200-300 mg theobromine per 40 g.

notes:
Theobromine (3,7-dimethylxanthine) - the principal methylxanthine of cocoa and chocolate, a mild adenosine-antagonist stimulant/diuretic/vasodilator weaker than caffeine. Curated as a clean LINEAR one-compartment oral compound from Lelo et al. 1986 (the disposition, measured by dosing theobromine directly in the same six healthy volunteers used for the caffeine-metabolite study) and Baggott et al. 2013 (oral absorption / Tmax and dose context).

WHY THEOBROMINE - the chocolate compound, and the counterpart to caffeine's metabolism. Theobromine is what most people actually consume the most of from cocoa (dark chocolate carries ~200-300 mg theobromine vs ~25-35 mg caffeine per 40 g). It is a slower, gentler xanthine than caffeine: lower clearance (1.20 vs 2.07 mL/min/kg) and a longer half-life (~7 h vs ~4-5 h), so it lingers - the pharmacological basis of chocolate's mild, prolonged, low-key stimulant character.

THE CAFFEINE CROSS-REFERENCE. Theobromine is ALSO one of caffeine's three primary demethylated metabolites (caffeine -> paraxanthine ~80% / theobromine ~11% / theophylline ~4%; Lelo 1986). The engine models any number of parallel metabolites, so in caffeine.json all three demethylation products are drawn - theobromine among them, as a ~10% (mass-fm) dashed line off the caffeine parent. This standalone entry is theobromine approached from the OTHER direction: the same molecule and the same Lelo-measured disposition (t½ 7.2 h, Vd 0.75 L/kg - byte-identical between the two files, so they never disagree), entered as its own dietary compound (eaten as cocoa) rather than formed from caffeine. Both appearances are consistent by construction.

NO METABOLITE LINE (a clean feature, not a gap). Theobromine is further metabolized (demethylation to 7-methylxanthine and 3-methylxanthine, and oxidation to 3,7-dimethyluric acid) and partly excreted unchanged, but no single downstream product dominates with a citable fraction-formed, so no metabolite is drawn - the same posture as pregabalin and lithium (renally-cleared / no-dominant-metabolite compounds). Modelling the parent alone is faithful.

LINEARITY. Unlike its isomer theophylline - which is EXCLUDED from the set for capacity-limited (Michaelis-Menten) elimination at therapeutic concentrations (see docs/DATA_GUIDE.md) - theobromine's kinetics are first-order and dose-proportional over ordinary dietary/studied doses (250-1000 mg, Baggott 2013), so superposition holds and linear: true. This makes the theobromine/theophylline pair a neat teaching contrast: two dimethylxanthine isomers, one linear (shipped) and one nonlinear (excluded).

SOFT INPUTS AND MAGNITUDE. The disposition rests on Lelo's directly-measured, self-consistent clearance and half-life (Vd = CL.t½/ln2, the cefotaxime posture - a coherent one-compartment set rather than a separately reported Vd). No single clean single-dose Cmax was sourced for an independent magnitude anchor, so the magnitude check is the built engine curve against the F.D/V ceiling and the known dietary range: a 500 mg oral dose gives an F.D/V ceiling of ~9.5 mg/L and a built engine oral peak of 7.1 mg/L at 3 h - below the ceiling and in the range consistent with plasma theobromine after a strong dark-chocolate / pure-theobromine dose. Oral only (the dietary route; theobromine's historical IV diuretic use has no clean modern single-population disposition beyond this oral dataset). EDUCATIONAL ILLUSTRATION ONLY - not for dosing.
